title: Smoky Spiced Turkish Lamb Adana Kebap
description: A nutty and aerated Turkish dish featuring garam masala, this Adana Kebap is perfect for a weeknight dinner, and its use of pressure cooking and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients makes it a great option for a quick and healthy meal
instructions: Soak the banana leaves in water for 30 minutes to make them pliable
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, garam masala, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld
Cut the banana leaves into 6-8 inch pieces and place a small amount of the lamb mixture onto the center of each leaf
Fold the leaves over the filling to form a tight cylinder and secure with a toothpick if necessary
Place the Adana Kebap into a pressure cooker and cook for 10 minutes
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure
Serve the Adana Kebap hot with a squeeze of lemon juice
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired
